title: Polestar Q1 2026 Earnings: Growth Masks Profitability Crisis
text:
Polestar reported mixed results for Q1 2026, with vehicle deliveries rising but financial performance deteriorating sharply. The Swedish EV maker delivered 13,126 vehicles, up 7% year-over-year, yet revenue remained essentially flat at $633 million versus $632 million in Q1 2025. Favorable currency movements in euros and pounds helped offset significant downward pressure on vehicle pricing.

The company faced severe profitability headwinds. Gross margin swung dramatically to negative 3.2% from positive 10.3% in the prior-year quarter. Contributing factors included intensified market pricing pressure, the impact of newly implemented EU and U.S. tariffs, and a shifting product mix. Polestar's net loss expanded to $383 million from $166 million year-over-year, more than doubled. Adjusted EBITDA deteriorated to a loss of $235 million compared to $96 million previously. The company's cash position eroded significantly, declining from $1,159 million at year-end 2025 to $676 million by March 31, 2026. Tariffs and aggressive industry pricing competition have emerged as primary headwinds threatening the automaker's path to profitability despite growing sales volumes.
